design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore constrained world map paths → Enter dungeons with action-adventure puzzle mechanics → Engage visible enemies in turn-based battles → Defeat boss to unlock next story gate
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Accessibility vs. depth; simplified mechanics to attract entry-level players while maintaining minimal RPG complexity
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: A young hero (Benjamin) reclaiming stolen elemental crystals to save the world; emphasis on action and exploration rather than party management
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical departure from FF series conventions: eliminates random encounters, save points (free saves), manual equipment management, and traditional party system. Explicitly designed as entry-level RPG. Incorporates action-adventure elements (jumping, tool use) as core exploration mechanics. Linear progression with no free-roaming world map—travels only along predetermined paths between 'icons' (towns/dungeons/battlefields)

difficulty.continues_system: Player can continue after game over by restarting the battle, but attack power suffers temporarily as penalty
difficulty.checkpointing: Free saving at any time during exploration (no save point restriction); battles are discrete encounters with continue option
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited agency; can avoid visible stationary enemies on field, but no difficulty modes documented. Continue system penalizes failure but allows progression

progression.upgrade_system: Automatic equipment replacement system: newly acquired armor automatically replaces current gear or upgrades weapon (e.g., steel sword → knight sword). No manual equipping. Statistics (vitality, attack, defense, speed, magic, accuracy, evasion) driven by experience points and leveling milestones
progression.unlock_mechanism: World map routes blocked by gray arrows; become accessible after completing specific dungeons. Battlefields must be 'cleaned out' (10 enemy encounters won per battlefield) to progress. Weapons/tools unlock exploration paths (axe for trees, bombs for seals, grappling hook for gaps)
replayability_hooks: Mystic Quest HardType ROM hack increases difficulty while preserving story and characters
Multiple dungeon routes with puzzle variations (e.g., ice pillar platform puzzles in Falls Basin)

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through dungeon design and tool-based exploration puzzles
Icon-based world structure (towns, dungeons, battlefields) visually organize narrative beats
Character interactions within town/dungeon icons drive plot progression

notable_bosses: Flamerus Rex (first crystal boss; documented in HardType hack description)

danger_signals: Larger-than-life enemy sprites during battles signal threat escalation
Blocked world map routes (gray arrows) indicate environmental dangers or story gates
environmental_storytelling: Tool-locked areas (axes on trees, bombs on seals, hooks on gaps) telegraph puzzle difficulty and progression gates
Battlefield icon clustering suggests danger zones vs. safe town areas
Crystal theft premise establishes elemental imbalance worldstate
